title: Twin roadside blasts kill 7 in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

Police confirmed both incidents were remotely detonated, with three individuals also sustaining injuries. Authorities have launched a search operation to apprehend those responsible for the deadly attacks.

title: How 33 lakh enumerators are counting India’s population
description: Census 2027 is India’s first fully digital Census and one of the largest data collection exercises in the world. But behind the technology are millions of enumerators, many of them government school teachers, working in extreme heat, facing safety concerns, connectivity issues, app glitches and resistance from residents. This report explores the challenges of conducting a nationwide population count and what it means for India’s future planning and policymaking.

Since taking charge, he's transformed into a dependable scorer, notching centuries at an impressive rate. This leadership role appears to have unlocked a new level, with his Test average nearly doubling and his run-scoring significantly boosted, raising questions about the impact of the armband on his game.

The incident involved two East Midlands Railway services heading towards London St Pancras, leading to a complete suspension of services.

Citizens can seek damages if right to walk is violated: SC
description: The Supreme Court has declared the right to walk a fundamental right, integral to life and free movement.  The apex court urged the government to enact a law ensuring well-demarcated footpaths on all roads, prioritizing pedestrian safety over motorized vehicles. This landmark ruling empowers citizens to seek legal recourse for violations and mandates civic bodies to maintain pedestrian infrastructure, transforming urban and rural living.

Nagpur NEET aspirant gets Abu Dhabi as exam centre
description: A Nagpur NEET aspirant received a shocking hall ticket assigning his exam centre to Abu Dhabi, over 2,500 km away, despite not possessing a passport. The National Testing Agency (NTA) assured rectification, but with the exam imminent, the family faced immense stress arranging travel. This incident follows widespread reports of exam centre changes, even for students who opted to retain their original locations, adding to the anxiety post-paper leak.

For 25 years, Meena supported her family, including her son's wife's fertility treatment. Friends recall her mentioning the doctor's increasingly watchful behavior. Her family questions why they weren't informed of his alleged mental health issues, leaving her in a dangerous situation.

title: Punjabi teen arrested for killing parents and grandmother in US
description: A 19-year-old of Punjabi origin, Gaurav Chopra, has been arrested in Texas for allegedly killing his parents and grandmother. The incident occurred just months after the family visited their native village in India. His brother narrowly escaped the attack, reporting Gaurav fled the scene. Investigators are seeking a motive for the shocking crime.

title: Jumping spider-inspired 3D camera uses under 1W power, may power robots and drones
description: Scientists have developed a new 3D camera, SpiderCam, inspired by jumping spiders' vision. This low-power camera, using less than one watt, estimates depth by comparing image focus differences. It could enable battery-powered robots, drones, and wearable devices to understand their surroundings efficiently.
